common

Class SequentialByteArrayReader

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.SequentialByteArrayReader
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ByteStreamReader, java.io.Closeable, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public class SequentialByteArrayReader
extends java.lang.Object
implements ByteStreamReader
    Performs sequential reading of primitive data types from a byte array.

    Supports reading of signed and unsigned integers, floating-point numbers, and byte sequences with configurable byte order (big-endian or little-endian).

    Since:
    12 December 2025
    Version:
    1.2
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field Description
      private int baseIndex  
      private byte[] buffer  
      private long bufferIndex  
      private java.nio.ByteOrder byteOrder  
      private java.util.Deque<java.lang.Long> markPosition  

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array using big-endian byte order.
      SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array using the specified byte order.
      SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf, int offset)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using big-endian byte order.
      SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf, int offset, java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using the specified byte order.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void close()
      Closes this reader.
      private byte getByte(long position)
      Returns a single byte from the array at the specified relative position.
      java.nio.ByteOrder getByteOrder()
      Returns the byte order used when interpreting multi-byte values.
      private byte[] getBytes(long position, int length)
      Copies and returns a sub-array from the byte array, starting from the specified position.
      long getCurrentPosition()
      Returns the current read position.
      java.nio.file.Path getPath()
      Currently not used and returns null.
      boolean hasRemaining(int n)
      Checks if at least the specified number of bytes are available to read.
      long length()
      Returns the readable length of this reader.
      void mark()
      Marks the current position in the buffer, allowing a subsequent reset() call to return to this position.
      byte peek(long offset)
      Retrieves the data at the specified offset within the byte array without advancing the current position.
      byte[] peek(long offset, int length)
      Retrieves a sub-array of bytes from the specified absolute offset without advancing the current position.
      byte readByte()
      Reads a single byte from the current position and advances the reader.
      byte[] readBytes(int length)
      Reads a sequence of bytes from the current position and advances the reader.
      double readDouble()
      Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value from the current position and advances the reader.
      float readFloat()
      Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value from the current position and advances the reader.
      int readInteger()
      Reads a 32-bit signed integer from the current position and advances the reader.
      long readLong()
      Reads a 64-bit signed long from the current position and advances the reader.
      short readShort()
      Reads a 16-bit signed short value from the current position and advances the reader.
      java.lang.String readString()
      Reads a null-terminated string (ISO-8859-1).
      int readUnsignedByte()
      Reads an 8-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the reader.
      int readUnsignedInt24()
      Reads a 24-bit unsigned integer (3 bytes) from the current position.
      long readUnsignedInteger()
      Reads a 32-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the reader.
      int readUnsignedShort()
      Reads a 16-bit unsigned short from the current position and advances the reader.
      private long readValue(int numBytes)
      Retrieves a value based on the number of bytes from the current position.
      long remaining()
      Returns the number of unread bytes remaining in the buffer, relative to the current read position.
      void reset()
      Resets the reader's position to the last marked position.
      void seek(long pos)
      Moves to the specified position within the byte array.
      void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Sets the byte order for interpreting the input bytes.
      void skip(long n)
      Skips forward by the given number of bytes.
      private void validateByteIndex(long position, int length)
      Checks whether the specified position is within the byte array’s bounds.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • buffer

        private final byte[] buffer

      • baseIndex

        private final int baseIndex

      • markPosition

        private final java.util.Deque<java.lang.Long> markPosition

      • bufferIndex

        private long bufferIndex

      • byteOrder

        private java.nio.ByteOrder byteOrder

    • Constructor Detail

      • SequentialByteArrayReader

        public SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf,
                                 int offset,
                                 java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        offset - the starting position within the byte array
        order - the byte order for interpreting the input bytes, using either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the byte array is null or empty
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if offset is negative or greater than the length of the byte array
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if order is null

      • SequentialByteArrayReader

        public SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array using big-endian byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array

      • SequentialByteArrayReader

        public SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf,
                                 java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array using the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        order - the byte order for interpreting the input bytes, using either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • SequentialByteArrayReader

        public SequentialByteArrayReader(byte[] buf,
                                 int offset)
        Constructs a reader for the input byte array, starting at the given offset and using big-endian byte order.
        Parameters:
        buf - the source byte array
        offset - the starting position within the byte array

    • Method Detail

      • close

        public void close()
        Closes this reader. No action is performed because this reader operates entirely on an in-memory byte array.
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.io.Closeable
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable

      • getPath

        public java.nio.file.Path getPath()
        Currently not used and returns null. This method is implemented solely to satisfy the ByteStreamReader interface contract.
        Specified by:
        getPath in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        null

      • setByteOrder

        public void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Sets the byte order for interpreting the input bytes.
        Specified by:
        setByteOrder in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        order - the byte order for interpreting the input bytes

      • getByteOrder

        public java.nio.ByteOrder getByteOrder()
        Returns the byte order used when interpreting multi-byte values.
        Specified by:
        getByteOrder in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • length

        public long length()
        Returns the readable length of this reader.
        Specified by:
        length in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the number of bytes from the configured base offset to the end of the byte array

      • getCurrentPosition

        public long getCurrentPosition()
        Returns the current read position.
        Specified by:
        getCurrentPosition in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the current position relative to the configured base offset

      • skip

        public void skip(long n)
        Skips forward by the given number of bytes.
        Specified by:
        skip in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        n - the number of bytes to skip
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the position is outside the valid range [0..length()]

      • seek

        public void seek(long pos)
        Moves to the specified position within the byte array.
        Specified by:
        seek in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        pos - the position to move to
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the position is invalid

      • mark

        public void mark()
        Marks the current position in the buffer, allowing a subsequent reset() call to return to this position.
        Specified by:
        mark in interface ByteStreamReader

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Resets the reader's position to the last marked position.
        Specified by:
        reset in interface ByteStreamReader
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the mark stack is empty

      • peek

        public byte peek(long offset)
        Retrieves the data at the specified offset within the byte array without advancing the current position.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        offset - the offset (relative to baseIndex)
        Returns:
        the byte of data

      • peek

        public byte[] peek(long offset,
                   int length)
        Retrieves a sub-array of bytes from the specified absolute offset without advancing the current position.
        Specified by:
        peek in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        offset - the position relative to the configured base offset
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the data

      • readByte

        public byte readByte()
        Reads a single byte from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readByte in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the byte value

      • readBytes

        public byte[] readBytes(int length)
        Reads a sequence of bytes from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readBytes in interface ByteStreamReader
        Parameters:
        length - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the data

      • readUnsignedByte

        public int readUnsignedByte()
        Reads an 8-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedByte in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the unsigned 8-bit value (0-255)

      • readShort

        public short readShort()
        Reads a 16-bit signed short value from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readShort in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the short value

      • readUnsignedShort

        public int readUnsignedShort()
        Reads a 16-bit unsigned short from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedShort in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the unsigned short value (0-65535) represented by an integer

      • readInteger

        public int readInteger()
        Reads a 32-bit signed integer from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readInteger in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the signed integer value

      • readUnsignedInteger

        public long readUnsignedInteger()
        Reads a 32-bit unsigned integer from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInteger in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the unsigned integer value represented as a long

      • readUnsignedInt24

        public int readUnsignedInt24()
        Reads a 24-bit unsigned integer (3 bytes) from the current position.
        Specified by:
        readUnsignedInt24 in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the 24-bit value as an integer

      • readLong

        public long readLong()
        Reads a 64-bit signed long from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readLong in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the long value

      • readFloat

        public float readFloat()
        Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision floating-point value from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readFloat in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the floating-point value

      • readDouble

        public double readDouble()
        Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point value from the current position and advances the reader.
        Specified by:
        readDouble in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the floating-point value

      • readString

        public java.lang.String readString()
        Reads a null-terminated string (ISO-8859-1).

        Scans for 0x00 starting from the current position. The pointer is advanced past the null terminator.

        Specified by:
        readString in interface ByteStreamReader
        Returns:
        the decoded string excluding the null terminator
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the end of the buffer is reached before a null terminator

      • getByte

        private byte getByte(long position)
        Returns a single byte from the array at the specified relative position.
        Parameters:
        position - the index (relative to base offset) in the byte array
        Returns:
        the byte fetched from the position

      • getBytes

        private byte[] getBytes(long position,
                        int length)
        Copies and returns a sub-array from the byte array, starting from the specified position.
        Parameters:
        position - the index (relative to base offset) in the byte array
        length - the total number of bytes to include in the sub-array (must be <= Integer.MAX_VALUE)
        Returns:
        a new byte array containing the subset of the original array

      • validateByteIndex

        private void validateByteIndex(long position,
                               int length)
        Checks whether the specified position is within the byte array’s bounds. If the position is out of range, an IndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown.
        Parameters:
        position - the position relative to the configured base offset (0 represents the first readable byte)
        length - the total number of bytes to check (must be <= Integer.MAX_VALUE)
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the position is out of bounds

      • remaining

        public long remaining()
        Returns the number of unread bytes remaining in the buffer, relative to the current read position.
        Returns:
        the number of bytes still available for reading

      • hasRemaining

        public boolean hasRemaining(int n)
        Checks if at least the specified number of bytes are available to read.
        Parameters:
        n - the number of bytes to check for
        Returns:
        true if n bytes or more remain, otherwise false

      • readValue

        private long readValue(int numBytes)
        Retrieves a value based on the number of bytes from the current position.
        Parameters:
        numBytes - the number of bytes to read
        Returns:
        the computed value as a long
